title: Neural Turing Machines
authors: Alex Graves, Greg Wayne, Ivo Danihelka
hook: A neural network learns to read and write from its own external memory.
promise: You will understand how neural networks can learn to use memory like a computer.
prerequisite_note: You only need a sense for how neural networks process data step by step.

glossary.plain: A storage matrix that sits outside the neural network, which the network can access and modify.
A component that learns how to extract information from the external memory.
A component that learns how to store or change information in the external memory.
A property that allows the whole system to be trained with gradient descent, since every operation is smooth and tunable.
A process that lets the network focus on specific parts of memory, rather than all at once.
A way to access memory by matching patterns, not just by fixed location.

cards.title: When networks learn to remember
body: Discover how a neural network can learn to use its own memory like a computer.

cards.title: How could a network learn like a computer?
body: Imagine if a neural network could write notes to itself, then read them back later to solve harder problems.
This is the leap Neural Turing Machines make: adding a flexible, trainable memory that the network can control.

cards.title: A network meets a memory bank
body: A neural network receives input and decides what to store or retrieve from an external memory matrix.
It uses special heads that learn to read and write, guided by attention over memory slots.
This lets the system learn algorithms like copying, sorting, or recalling associations from examples.
cards.caption: The network learns to control memory access, enabling algorithmic reasoning.

cards.title: How does memory change learning?
body: Toggle to see how a plain network compares to one with an external memory bank.
cards.offNote: Struggles to remember long sequences or learn complex tasks.
cards.onNote: Can learn to store, retrieve, and manipulate information over many steps.

cards.title: A neural network with a memory tape
body: The key move is to give a neural network a memory matrix, plus the ability to learn how to use it.
By making memory access differentiable, the whole system can be trained end-to-end with gradient descent, just like any neural net.

cards.title: How much memory access is needed?
body: Use the slider to see what happens as the network's memory size increases.
cards.low: Network can only solve very simple problems.
cards.mid: Network can copy or sort short sequences.
cards.high: Network can learn more complex algorithms and remember longer sequences.

cards.title: What goes into a Neural Turing Machine?
body: Pick the six main ingredients that make this memory-augmented network work.

choices.label: Controller Network
choices.note: Decides how to read and write memory.

choices.label: Read Head
choices.note: Extracts data from chosen memory locations.

choices.label: Write Head
choices.note: Modifies or adds to the memory matrix.

choices.label: Attention Mechanism
choices.note: Focuses the heads on specific memory slots.

choices.label: Differentiable Addressing
choices.note: Makes read and write operations trainable with gradients.

cards.title: Learning algorithms from examples
body: NTMs could learn to copy sequences, sort data, and perform associative recall just from input-output pairs.
Compared to standard recurrent networks, they handled longer sequences and more complex tasks with fewer failures.

bars.label: Copy task accuracy
bars.note: NTM nearly perfect, RNN much lower

bars.label: Associative recall
bars.note: NTM retrieves correct items more often

cards.caption: The NTM solved tasks that standard RNNs could not, especially as sequence length increased.

cards.title: Why this paper changed the field
body: Neural Turing Machines showed that neural networks can learn algorithmic tasks if given the right memory tools.
This idea inspired new architectures and made neural networks feel more like programmable computers, not just pattern matchers.

cards.title: Neural Turing Machines in four lines

recap.label: Attach
recap.note: Connect a memory bank to a neural network

recap.label: Control
recap.note: Let the network learn to read and write memory

recap.label: Train
recap.note: Use differentiable operations so gradient descent works

recap.label: Solve
recap.note: Learn algorithms like copying and sorting from examples

cards.title: Neural Turing Machines
body: Read the original paper for full details and experiments on learning with memory.
